name: Automated Sentinel
oracle_text:
Malfunction 3 (This permanent enters tapped with three malfunction counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)
Hexproof
When ~ no longer has malfunction counters on it, draw a card.
flavor_text: Three centuries of dormancy in the vaults beneath Mount Rendon. When power surged through its crystalline matrices, it remembered only one directive: observe.

validation_errors: Line 1: "This permanent" should use "~" or "this" only in specific MTG contexts
Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(This permanent enters tapped with three malfunction counters on it. At the beginning of your upkeep, remove a malfunction counter from it.)"
Combined oracle + flavor text is 441 characters, exceeding the 350-char limit for this card type

art_prompt: A towering stainless steel construct with a cylindrical head crowned by a crystalline eye, its armored frame layered with hydraulic hoses and mismatched salvaged plating. Dormant and motionless, it stands upright in a shadowed underground vault carved into stone, faint crystalline light emanating from its core.. Stylized digital illustration, geometric composition, cool steel-blue and teal palette, screen-glow lighting, post-apocalyptic science-fantasy setting.
design_notes: Blue rare artifact creature designed as a strong payoff for the Malfunction mechanic. Hexproof provides protection during the three-turn ramp-up phase, and drawing a card when fully online rewards players for keeping it alive. The 3/3 body for CMC 2 is justified by: (1) three turns of delay before becoming untapped and threatening, (2) vulnerability to artifact removal during the setup window, (3) hexproof creating counterplay restrictions. Fits U/W (Ancient Technology) and U/R (Mad Science) archetypes.
